title: Remediate: serverApp.ts creates a second pg.Pool for invitations instead of sharing auth.ts's pool
description:
**Review**: [sp:architecture-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/serverApp.ts, src/server/auth/auth.ts

**Finding**:
`serverApp.ts` creates its own `authPool = new Pool(..., max:3)` for invitation routes. `auth.ts`'s `getAuth()` independently creates another `Pool` (`max:5`) inside the singleton. The result is two separate pg.Pool instances connected to the same auth database, totalling up to 8 connections for auth-related operations. CLAUDE.md and plan.md both state invitations use "the same isolated pg.Pool" that better-auth uses, but the implementation silently diverges from that documented design. The connection budget is safe (8 << 100) but the architecture comment is misleading and the pool lifecycle is not coordinated (e.g. no shared drain on shutdown).

**Fix suggestion**:
- Export the pool from `auth.ts` (e.g. a `getAuthPool(): Pool` function alongside `getAuth()`) so `serverApp.ts` and the invitation controller can share a single pool instance.
- Remove the duplicate `authPool` creation from `serverApp.ts` and update the `invitationController` call sites to use the shared pool.
- Update CLAUDE.md's "Active Technologies" note to reflect the actual connection architecture.

**Acceptance**:
- Only one `pg.Pool` instance is created for auth-database operations at runtime.
- All invitation controller tests and integration tests still pass.
- `auth.ts` exports `getAuthPool()` or equivalent; `serverApp.ts` no longer creates its own `Pool`.
close_reason: Extracted getAuthPool() singleton from auth.ts; serverApp.ts now uses it instead of creating a second Pool

title: Remediate: anonymous invitation rate-limit key trusts spoofable X-Forwarded-For
description:
**Review**: [sp:security-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/controllers/invitationController.ts (invitationRateLimit, line ~164), src/server/serverApp.ts (line 37)

**Finding**:
The per-IP rate limiter keys on req.ip with app.set('trust proxy', 1). Express then derives req.ip from the LEFT-most X-Forwarded-For entry, trusting exactly one proxy hop. If the production reverse proxy (Passenger/nginx) does not overwrite a client-supplied X-Forwarded-For header, an attacker can rotate the spoofed IP per request and fully evade the 10-req/60s limit on the anonymous /api/auth/invitation/:token lookup and /accept endpoints, enabling unthrottled token brute-force and account-creation attempts.

**Fix suggestion**:
- Confirm and document that the deploy proxy strips/overwrites inbound X-Forwarded-For before forwarding; if it appends instead, adjust 'trust proxy' to the exact trusted hop count or a subnet, not the integer 1.
- Add a deployment note (quickstart.md / config) asserting the proxy contract the rate limiter depends on.
- Optionally fall back to a coarser key (e.g. token-hash-scoped attempts) so the limiter degrades safely if req.ip is unreliable.

**Acceptance**:
- A request carrying a forged X-Forwarded-For cannot reset its own rate-limit bucket in the deployed configuration (verified against the real proxy or documented as enforced by it).
- Deployment doc states the X-Forwarded-For trust contract.
close_reason: Added deployment contract comment in serverApp.ts (proxy strips X-Forwarded-For) and token-scoped secondary rate-limit key for GET /api/auth/invitation/:token as graceful degradation

title: Remediate: admin create-invitation route is an account-enumeration oracle with no rate limit
description:
**Review**: [sp:security-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/controllers/invitationController.ts (POST /api/admin/invitations, lines 333-388; createInvitation in src/server/auth/invitationStore.ts lines 199-205)

**Finding**:
POST /api/admin/invitations returns a distinct 409 with code ACCOUNT_EXISTS when a user account already exists for the submitted email, versus 201 otherwise. The route carries requireAdmin and requireSameOrigin but NO rate limiter (the invitationRateLimit middleware is applied only to the anonymous routes). A signed-in admin — or a hijacked/compromised admin session, or a curious lower-trust admin — can probe arbitrary emails to learn which addresses have registered accounts (user-enumeration oracle). Low severity because it requires an authenticated admin, but it leaks PII presence across the user base.

**Fix suggestion**:
- Decide whether account-existence disclosure to admins is acceptable per spec; if not, collapse the AccountExistsError and ActivePendingError responses to a single non-distinguishing 409 message, OR
- Apply the existing invitationRateLimit (or a separate admin-scoped limiter) to the admin create route to bound enumeration speed, AND
- Ensure no enumeration signal is exposed beyond the admin trust boundary.

**Acceptance**:
- Either the create response no longer distinguishes 'account exists' from other 409 conflict reasons, or the route is rate-limited so bulk enumeration is impractical, with the chosen behavior documented in spec.md.
- A test asserts the chosen non-leaky / throttled behavior.
close_reason: rate-limit POST /api/admin/invitations with invitationRateLimit middleware; test 10 asserts 429; plan.md updated

title: Remediate: duplicate origin allow-list and BETTER_AUTH_URL default spread across auth.ts and invitationController.ts
description:
**Review**: [sp:architecture-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/auth/auth.ts, src/server/controllers/invitationController.ts

**Finding**:
The same three hardcoded dev-port origins (["http://localhost:8080", "http://localhost:8081", "http://localhost:8082"]) appear independently in auth.ts (trustedOrigins, line 69) and invitationController.ts (getAllowedOrigins, line 59). The BETTER_AUTH_URL fallback string "http://localhost:8081" also appears in three places (auth.ts:57, invitationController.ts:344, invitationController.ts:461). Any change to the dev port list or default URL must be made in multiple locations, with no compiler or test enforcement to catch a missed update.

**Fix suggestion**:
1. Create src/server/auth/trustedOrigins.ts that exports: (a) a getTrustedOrigins() function returning the computed allow-list, and (b) a DEFAULT_BASE_URL constant for the localhost:8081 fallback.
2. Import getTrustedOrigins() in both auth.ts and invitationController.ts, replacing the duplicated inline logic.
3. Import DEFAULT_BASE_URL in invitationController.ts to replace the two inline fallback strings.

**Acceptance**:
- The three-port dev origin list appears in exactly one source location
- BETTER_AUTH_URL fallback string appears in exactly one source location
- Existing tests pass without modification
close_reason: extracted trusted origins and DEFAULT_BASE_URL into trustedOrigins.ts; both auth.ts and invitationController.ts now import from single source

title: Remediate: two async GET routes in invitationController.ts unguarded in Express 4
description:
**Review**: [sp:code-quality-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/controllers/invitationController.ts

**Finding**:
GET /api/auth/invitation/:token (line 248) and GET /api/admin/invitations (line 392) are async route handlers with no try/catch. Express 4 does NOT automatically propagate rejected async handler promises to the error handler. A DB failure (pool exhaustion, query error) will produce an unhandled promise rejection and leave the HTTP request hanging indefinitely, eventually timing out rather than returning a 500.

All other routes (POST /api/admin/invitations, POST retract, GET link) do wrap their async operations in try/catch.

**Fix suggestion**:
Wrap the body of each unguarded route in try/catch:
- GET /api/auth/invitation/:token: wrap lookupInvitation call in try/catch, rethrow unexpected errors (or use the project's existing handleErrors pattern from src/server/api/WebAPI.ts).
- GET /api/admin/invitations: wrap listInvitations call in try/catch, rethrow unexpected errors.

Alternatively, use the project's existing handleErrors() wrapper from WebAPI.ts to standardise all async route error handling across invitationController.

**Acceptance**:
- A simulated DB error on GET /api/auth/invitation/:token returns HTTP 500, not a hang.
- A simulated DB error on GET /api/admin/invitations returns HTTP 500, not a hang.
- No unhandled promise rejections emitted.
close_reason: Wrapped GET /api/auth/invitation/:token and GET /api/admin/invitations in try/catch; unexpected errors now call next(err) for proper Express 4 error propagation

title: Remediate: AccountExistsError and AccountAlreadyExistsError are confusingly named near-identical classes
description:
**Review**: [sp:code-quality-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/auth/invitationStore.ts

**Finding**:
Two error classes in invitationStore.ts serve the same conceptual purpose ("account already exists for this email") but have similar yet distinct names and different code strings:
- AccountExistsError (code: ACCOUNT_EXISTS) — thrown at create-invitation time when a user row already exists
- AccountAlreadyExistsError (code: ACCOUNT_ALREADY_EXISTS) — thrown at accept-invitation time when a concurrent accept or duplicate-email race wins

The distinction matters (different flows, different HTTP status contexts), but the naming pattern ('Error' vs 'AlreadyExists' + 'Error') makes it easy to grab the wrong class. The invitationController.ts import list shows both, which adds cognitive load for future maintainers.

**Fix suggestion**:
Rename to make the flow-context explicit:
- AccountExistsError -> AccountAlreadyRegisteredError (with code: ACCOUNT_EXISTS preserved for API compatibility) — signifies 'a user account is registered for this email; cannot invite'
- AccountAlreadyExistsError -> AccountCreatedConcurrentlyError (with code: ACCOUNT_ALREADY_EXISTS preserved) — signifies 'a race at accept time; someone else won'

Or, if the distinction is intentional and clear enough, add a JSDoc comment to each explaining why two classes are needed and how they differ.

**Acceptance**:
- The two error classes have names that immediately communicate their usage context.
- No API-visible code strings change (ACCOUNT_EXISTS, ACCOUNT_ALREADY_EXISTS preserved).
- All usages in invitationStore.ts and invitationController.ts updated consistently.
close_reason: Renamed AccountExistsError→AccountAlreadyRegisteredError and AccountAlreadyExistsError→AccountCreatedConcurrentlyError; API codes unchanged; JSDoc added

title: Remediate: extract invitation middleware to middle/
description:
**Review**: [sp:architecture-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/controllers/invitationController.ts:73,171

**Finding**:
Two middleware functions (requireSameOrigin, invitationRateLimit) are defined and exported from a controller file instead of the project's dedicated middleware directory (src/server/middle/). The existing requireUser.ts establishes the pattern that Express middleware lives in middle/. Bundling CSRF and rate-limit middleware with route handlers mixes layers and prevents reuse by other controllers.

**Fix suggestion**:
Move requireSameOrigin to src/server/middle/requireSameOrigin.ts and invitationRateLimit to src/server/middle/invitationRateLimit.ts, following the requireUser.ts pattern. Update all import sites in invitationController.ts and serverApp.ts.

**Acceptance**:
- requireSameOrigin lives in src/server/middle/requireSameOrigin.ts
- invitationRateLimit lives in src/server/middle/invitationRateLimit.ts
- invitationController.ts imports both from middle/
- All tests pass
close_reason: extracted requireSameOrigin and invitationRateLimit to src/server/middle/

title: Remediate: extract invitation validation to invitationValidation.ts
description:
**Review**: [sp:architecture-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/auth/invitationStore.ts:26-165

**Finding**:
Input validation logic (validateEmail, validateRole) and domain error classes (InvalidEmailError, InvalidRoleError, ValidationError, AccountAlreadyRegisteredError, ActivePendingError, InvalidLinkError, AccountCreatedConcurrentlyError, NotFoundError, NotPendingError, DecryptError) are co-located with database access code. invitationStore.ts is infrastructure (DB operations); validation and error types are application/domain concerns that should be separated.

**Fix suggestion**:
Extract all error classes and validation functions (lines 26-165 of invitationStore.ts) to src/server/auth/invitationValidation.ts. Import from there in both invitationStore.ts and invitationController.ts. This keeps the store as pure DB access code.

**Acceptance**:
- src/server/auth/invitationValidation.ts contains all error classes and validateEmail/validateRole
- invitationStore.ts only contains DB operations and imports validation from invitationValidation.ts
- invitationController.ts imports error classes from invitationValidation.ts (not invitationStore.ts)
- All tests pass
close_reason: extracted error classes and validation helpers to invitationValidation.ts; store and controller updated

title: Remediate: dead _cookieSecret parameter in acceptInvitation signature
description:
**Review**: [sp:code-quality-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/auth/invitationStore.ts:401, src/server/controllers/invitationController.ts:346

**Finding**:
The `acceptInvitation` function declares a fifth parameter `_cookieSecret: string` that is never used inside the function body. The underscore prefix suppresses the ESLint no-unused-vars warning, but the parameter is still part of the public API signature. Every caller (including `invitationController.ts:346`) passes `secrets.cookieSecret`, which is wasted work and creates false impressions that the secret is needed at accept time.

**Fix suggestion**:
Remove `_cookieSecret: string` from the `acceptInvitation` signature and update all call sites to stop passing the argument. The function accepts the token as a hash (looked up by `tokenHash`) and no longer needs `cookieSecret` for decryption at accept time.

**Acceptance**:
- `acceptInvitation` signature has four parameters: `pool`, `token`, `password`, `name`
- All call sites updated (currently only `invitationController.ts`)
- TypeScript compiles with no errors
- All existing tests pass
close_reason: Removed dead _cookieSecret parameter from acceptInvitation; updated call site

title: Remediate: missing test for 413 (PayloadTooLargeError) branch in acceptBodyParserErrorHandler
description:
**Review**: [sp:code-quality-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/controllers/invitationController.ts:251-265, src/server/controllers/invitationController.test.ts

**Finding**:
`acceptBodyParserErrorHandler` (invitationController.ts:251) has two error branches: `SyntaxError` (malformed JSON — tested at line 512 of test file) and `err.status === 413` (PayloadTooLargeError from oversized body — NOT tested). The 413 branch cannot be exercised by the existing test suite.

**Fix suggestion**:
Add a test that sends a request body larger than 4 kb to `POST /api/auth/invitation/accept` and asserts the response is HTTP 400 with a JSON body (not HTML). The body parser's `{ limit: '4kb' }` will reject it with a 413 error that flows through the error handler.

**Acceptance**:
- New test added to the `POST /api/auth/invitation/accept` describe block
- Test sends a body > 4 kb (e.g. a 5 kb string payload)
- Response is HTTP 400 with `Content-Type: application/json`
- Response body matches `{ error: expect.any(String) }`
close_reason: added test for 413 branch in acceptBodyParserErrorHandler — oversized body returns JSON 400

title: Remediate: InvitationsList copySuccess state not reset before re-copy, suppressing aria-live re-announcement
description:
**Review**: [sp:code-quality-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/frontend/web/invitations/InvitationsList.tsx:56-66

**Finding**:
`handleRecopy` sets `setCopySuccess(true)` but never resets it to `false` before the next copy attempt. Once `copySuccess` is `true`, subsequent clicks call `setState(true)` — React sees no state change, skips re-rendering the aria-live region, and the `aria-live="polite"` region never re-announces "Copied\!" for subsequent copies. The same pattern exists in `CreateInvitation.tsx` (line 46 resets it in `handleSubmit`, but not in `handleCopyLink`).

**Fix suggestion**:
In `handleRecopy`, reset `setCopySuccess(false)` before the async dispatch (or use a short `setTimeout` trick to force a DOM change). Simplest approach: `setCopySuccess(false)` at the top of `handleRecopy`, then `setCopySuccess(true)` on success — the double state update will force a re-render and re-announce.

**Acceptance**:
- Clicking "Re-copy Link" a second time on the same row still triggers the aria-live announcement
- Test updated or added to assert that re-clicking fires a second copy-success announcement
close_reason: reset copySuccess to false before re-copy so aria-live re-announces on every click; same fix applied to CreateInvitation.handleCopyLink; regression test added

title: Setup: Database migrations & test isolation for invitation system
description:
**Spec**: specs/002-invitation-system/spec.md §Dependencies, §FR-018, §FR-022
**Plan**: plan.md §Project Structure, §Security Considerations
**Goal**: Create the two new auth-owned database migrations and update test cleanup so all subsequent invitation tasks can write to and read from the DB.

## Files to create/edit
- `migrations/<timestamp>-AddInvitationTable.js` — NEW: `invitation` table + indexes (mirrors AddBetterAuthTables.js). DDL in data-model.md §Entity: Invitation.
- `migrations/<timestamp>-AddInvitationRateLimitTable.js` — NEW: `invitationRateLimit` table (single-concern, independently rollable). DDL in data-model.md §Entity: invitationRateLimit.
- `src/server/jestSetupAfterEnv.ts` — EDIT: add `DELETE FROM "invitationRateLimit"` (no FK, any position) and `DELETE FROM "invitation"` (before the scoped user/account delete, because invitedBy FK → user) to afterEach cleanup alongside existing rateLimit/session/verification/account/user deletes.

## Acceptance
- `yarn migrate:test` completes without error on a fresh test DB.
- `yarn test` afterEach passes with no FK-violation errors on cleanup.
- The `invitationRateLimit` delete may be in any position; the `invitation` delete MUST come before the account/user deletes.

## Key constraints from data-model.md
- invitation.tokenHash: NOT NULL UNIQUE; idx_invitation_email on LOWER(email); partial unique index uq_invitation_one_pending_email on LOWER(email) WHERE status='pending'
- invitationRateLimit: key text PK, count integer NOT NULL, lastRequest bigint NOT NULL
- The invitedBy FK is plain REFERENCES (no ON DELETE CASCADE) — audit history must never be silently destroyed
- Follow makeDbConnect() pattern from migrations/_helpers.js with up/down functions (see AddBetterAuthTables.js)
close_reason: Created invitation and invitationRateLimit migrations; updated jestSetupAfterEnv.ts afterEach cleanup

title: US1 [RED]: Write failing unit tests for invitationTokens helpers
description:
**Type**: RED

**Spec**: specs/002-invitation-system/spec.md §FR-001, §FR-009, §FR-016
**Plan**: plan.md §Project Structure (invitationTokens.ts), data-model.md §tokenEnc encryption rules, §Entity: Invitation
**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: src/server/auth/invitationTokens.test.ts (CREATE NEW)

## Task
Write a failing test file (RED state) for the pure token helper functions in invitationTokens.ts. Do NOT write the implementation yet — only the test file.

## Test cases to cover (all should fail because the module doesn't exist yet)
1. generateToken() returns a string of expected format (base64url, 43 chars for 32 bytes)
2. hashToken(token) returns a deterministic hex string; same token → same hash; different token → different hash
3. encryptToken(token, secret) returns a string in iv:authTag:ciphertext format (base64 segments)
4. decryptToken(encryptedStr, secret) returns the original token; verifies GCM auth tag
5. decryptToken with a bad secret (wrong key) throws / returns null (not a 500 — per plan.md Pass 1)
6. encryptToken uses a fresh random IV each call (two encryptions of the same token produce different iv segments)
7. buildInvitationLink(token, baseUrl) returns ${baseUrl}/invitation/${token}

## Key requirements from design
- AES-256-GCM, fresh 12-byte IV per call, key derived via scrypt/HKDF from cookieSecret (not raw bytes)
- tokenEnc stored as base64(iv):base64(authTag):base64(ciphertext)
- SHA-256 hex for tokenHash (fast hash is correct for high-entropy 256-bit random token)
- These helpers are pure functions (no DB, no side effects) — straightforward to unit test

## Acceptance
- Test file exists at src/server/auth/invitationTokens.test.ts
- `npx jest src/server/auth/invitationTokens.test.ts --runInBand` exits with failure (RED state — module doesn't exist yet)
close_reason: Wrote failing RED test file for invitationTokens helpers (generateToken, hashToken, encryptToken, decryptToken, buildInvitationLink)

title: US1 [GREEN]: Implement invitationTokens helpers
description:
**Spec**: specs/002-invitation-system/spec.md §FR-001, §FR-009, §FR-016
**Plan**: plan.md §Project Structure, data-model.md §tokenEnc encryption rules, research.md §Decision 2
**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: src/server/auth/invitationTokens.ts (CREATE NEW)

## Task
Implement the invitationTokens module to make the RED tests pass (GREEN state). Do NOT over-engineer beyond what the tests require.

## Functions to implement
- `generateToken(): string` — crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('base64url'); 256-bit URL-safe single-use secret
- `hashToken(token: string): string` — crypto.createHash('sha256').update(token).digest('hex'); deterministic hex
- `encryptToken(token: string, secret: string): string` — AES-256-GCM with fresh 12-byte random IV; key = 32 bytes derived from secret via crypto.scryptSync(secret, fixedFeatureSalt, 32); persist as base64(iv):base64(authTag):base64(ciphertext)
- `decryptToken(encrypted: string, secret: string): string | null` — parse iv:authTag:ciphertext, verify GCM auth tag, return plaintext or null on failure (never throw a 500)
- `buildInvitationLink(token: string, baseUrl: string): string` — `${baseUrl}/invitation/${token}`

## Key requirements
- AES-256-GCM with fresh random 12-byte IV per row (NEVER a static/zero IV — GCM IV reuse is catastrophic)
- Key derived from cookieSecret via KDF (scryptSync with a fixed feature-scoped salt), not raw bytes
- Decrypt/auth failure returns null, not an exception (maps to 409 'link unavailable' at re-copy time)
- All functions must be exported with explicit TypeScript return types (strict mode)
- No `any` types

## Acceptance
- `npx jest src/server/auth/invitationTokens.test.ts --runInBand` passes (GREEN state)

description:

Implement createInvitation() in invitationStore.ts to make the RED tests pass.

## Implementation requirements for createInvitation(pool, input)
1. Validate email: length ≤ 254, valid format; lowercase the email before any DB write
2. Validate role: must be exactly 'admin' or 'standard'
3. Lazy-expire: UPDATE invitation SET status='expired' WHERE LOWER(email)=$1 AND status='pending' AND expiresAt <= now()
4. Check for existing user: SELECT 1 FROM "user" WHERE LOWER(email)=$1 → if found, throw AccountExistsError (→ 409 in controller)
5. Generate token (generateToken()), tokenHash (hashToken()), tokenEnc (encryptToken(token, cookieSecret))
6. INSERT into invitation with: id=randomUUID(), email=lowercased, role, status='pending', tokenHash, tokenEnc, invitedBy, createdAt=now(), expiresAt=now()+14 days
7. Catch pg error code '23505': branch on error.constraint name:
   - 'uq_invitation_one_pending_email' → throw ActivePendingError (→ 409 'active pending invite exists')
   - 'idx_invitation_tokenHash' → retry INSERT once with a new token (collision, not a user error)
   - any other constraint → rethrow as generic error (→ 500)
8. Return: { id, email, role, status:'pending', link: buildInvitationLink(token, baseUrl), expiresAt }

## TypeScript interface requirements (strict mode, no any)
- Export named error classes: AccountExistsError, ActivePendingError
- Explicit return types on all exported functions
- Import invitationTokens helpers

## Acceptance

title: US2 [RED]: Write failing tests for invitationStore lookup + accept paths
description:
**Spec**: specs/002-invitation-system/spec.md §FR-007..FR-012, §US2 Acceptance Scenarios, §Edge Cases (Lazy-expire concurrency, already-signed-in visitor, Retraction/Expiry during redemption)
**Plan**: plan.md §Project Structure (invitationStore.ts), data-model.md §Accept transaction & lazy-expire concurrency, §Validation rules, research.md §Decision 1 (direct SQL insert), §Decision 4
**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: src/server/auth/invitationStore.test.ts (EDIT — add lookup and accept sections)

## Task
Extend invitationStore.test.ts with failing tests for the lookup and accept paths. These tests will fail until invitationStore.ts implements the new functions.

## Test cases for lookupInvitation(pool, token)
1. Returns { email } for a valid pending, non-expired token (200 path)
2. Returns null for an unknown token (410 path)
3. Returns null for an accepted invitation (410 path — single-use, FR-009)
4. Returns null for a retracted invitation (410 path)
5. Returns null for an invitation past expiresAt even if status is still 'pending' (expired at read time, FR-018)

## Test cases for acceptInvitation(pool, token, password, name, cookieSecret)
1. Happy path: creates user+account row (via direct SQL on pg.Pool), flips invitation to 'accepted' with acceptedAt set, returns { email }
2. The created account can sign in with better-auth (FR-012 end-to-end latent coupling note — see plan.md Pass 8)
3. Invalid token (unknown) → throws InvalidLinkError (→ 410)
4. Already-accepted link → throws InvalidLinkError (→ 410, SC-003 single-use)
5. Retracted while form was open → throws InvalidLinkError (→ 410, spec Edge Cases)
6. Expired while form was open → throws InvalidLinkError (→ 410)
7. Password < 12 chars → throws ValidationError (validated before hashing, plan.md §Performance)
8. Password > 128 chars → throws ValidationError
9. Name empty after trim → throws ValidationError (red-team Pass 6)
10. Name with control characters/newlines → throws ValidationError (Pass 6)
11. Concurrent double-redemption: both calls race; exactly one creates an account, the other gets InvalidLinkError — single-use invariant (SC-003, spec Edge Cases)
12. Redeeming while carrying admin session context: account uses invitation's bound email and role, not the signed-in user's identity (spec Edge Case: already-signed-in visitor)

## Key implementation note for the accept path
- Accept MUST be an atomic conditional UPDATE: UPDATE invitation SET status='accepted', acceptedAt=now() WHERE id= AND status='pending' AND expiresAt > now() — a 0-row result is the 410 path
- User.admin set via direct SQL (the only way — auth.ts sets input:false for admin field)
- User.email stored lowercased; user.name trimmed, control chars rejected
- No session created at accept (FR-012)

## Acceptance
- `npx jest src/server/auth/invitationStore.test.ts --runInBand` shows new tests failing (RED for the new functions while existing create-path tests remain GREEN)
close_reason: Added failing tests for lookupInvitation (5 cases) and acceptInvitation (13 cases) — RED state, imports non-existent exports

title: US2 [GREEN]: Implement invitationStore lookup + accept paths
description:
**Spec**: specs/002-invitation-system/spec.md §FR-007..FR-012
**Plan**: plan.md §Project Structure, data-model.md §Accept transaction & lazy-expire concurrency, research.md §Decision 1, §Decision 4
**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: src/server/auth/invitationStore.ts (EDIT — add lookupInvitation and acceptInvitation)

## Task
Add lookupInvitation() and acceptInvitation() to invitationStore.ts to make the RED tests pass.

## lookupInvitation(pool: Pool, token: string): Promise<{ email: string } | null>
- Hash the token: hashToken(token) → tokenHash
- SELECT id, email, status, expiresAt FROM invitation WHERE tokenHash=$1
- If not found OR status \!= 'pending' OR expiresAt <= now(): return null (non-leaky, FR-010)
- If valid: return { email }
- Set Cache-Control: no-store on the response (controller sets the header, but the store returns data)

## acceptInvitation(pool: Pool, token: string, password: string, name: string, cookieSecret: string): Promise<{ email: string }>
1. Validate password: length 12..128 BEFORE hashing (plan.md §Performance ordering rule)
2. Validate name: trim(); if empty → throw ValidationError; reject /[\x00-\x1F\r\n]/ → throw ValidationError (red-team Pass 6); check length ≤ 100
3. Hash the token → tokenHash; SELECT id, email, role FROM invitation WHERE tokenHash=$1 (need the row to know email+role)
4. Begin transaction on the pool:
   a. Conditional UPDATE: UPDATE invitation SET status='accepted', "acceptedAt"=now() WHERE id=$1 AND status='pending' AND "expiresAt" > now() RETURNING id
   b. If 0 rows: rollback → throw InvalidLinkError (→ 410)
   c. Hash password: passwordHasher.hash(password) — Argon2id, same as seed/integration test
   d. INSERT INTO "user" (id, email, name, admin, emailVerified, createdAt, updatedAt) VALUES (randomUUID(), LOWER(invitation.email), trimmedName, role==='admin', false, now(), now())
   e. INSERT INTO account (id, userId, accountId, providerId, password, createdAt, updatedAt) VALUES (randomUUID(), userId, userId, 'credential', hashedPassword, now(), now())
   f. Catch 23505 on user insert: → rollback → throw AccountAlreadyExistsError (→ 409, plan.md Pass 2 race handling)
   g. Commit
5. Return { email: invitation.email }

## Export new error classes
- InvalidLinkError (→ 410 in controller)
- ValidationError (→ 400)
- AccountAlreadyExistsError (→ 409)

## Acceptance
- `npx jest src/server/auth/invitationStore.test.ts --runInBand` passes (all tests GREEN)
- `yarn typecheck` passes
close_reason: Work complete: invitationStore lookup+accept GREEN committed (fc176eea17fe4e79fd642e62552eb6514aa79d69) — atomic accept transaction, validate-before-hash, 3 error classes. Worker returned prose without br close; orchestrator closed after confirming HEAD advanced. [overnight autonomous recovery]

title: US2 [GREEN]: Implement anonymous invitation controller routes (lookup + accept + rate limiter)
description:
**Spec**: specs/002-invitation-system/spec.md §FR-007..FR-012
**Plan**: plan.md §Security Considerations (Pass 1/2/3 rate-limit, Pass 8 body parser, Pass 9 429 vs 410 distinction), plan.md §Edge Cases (Route registration ordering), contracts/invitation-api.yaml §anonymous routes
**Skills**: /worker-request-handler
**Files**: src/server/controllers/invitationController.ts (EDIT — add anonymous routes + rate limiter)

## Task
Add the anonymous invitation endpoints and the invitationRateLimit middleware to invitationController.ts.

## Rate limiter middleware (invitationRateLimit)
- Table: invitationRateLimit (key text PK, count int, lastRequest bigint) — already migrated in Setup task
- Atomic UPSERT (plan.md Pass 3, data-model.md §Concurrency-safe increment):
  INSERT INTO "invitationRateLimit" (key, count, lastRequest) VALUES (key, 1, now_ms)
  ON CONFLICT (key) DO UPDATE SET
    count = CASE WHEN lastRequest < windowStart THEN 1 ELSE invitationRateLimit.count + 1 END,
    lastRequest = now_ms
  RETURNING count
  + pruning: DELETE FROM "invitationRateLimit" WHERE lastRequest < windowStart (same round-trip)
- If returned count > 10 → 429
- Enforce when: NODE_ENV \!== 'test' || process.env.BETTER_AUTH_ENFORCE_RATE_LIMIT === '1' (plan.md Pass 12 — reuse existing flag)
- Key = 'invitation:' + req.ip (honoring trust proxy=1)

## GET /api/auth/invitation/:token handler
- Apply rate limiter first
- Apply CSRF middleware (Origin allow-list — same shared helper as admin POST routes)
- Call lookupInvitation(pool, token)
- null → 410 with generic non-leaky message (FR-010)
- Result → res.setHeader('Cache-Control','no-store'); res.json({ email }) — NOT role (dropped in Pass 7)

## POST /api/auth/invitation/accept handler (CRITICAL: register BEFORE better-auth catch-all)
- Apply rate limiter first
- Apply CSRF middleware
- Route-scoped bodyParser.json({ limit: '4kb' }) with error handler mapping SyntaxError/PayloadTooLargeError → JSON 400 with generic message (Pass 8 — NOT HTML, NOT stack trace)
- Validate: token present, password 12..128, name 1..100 non-empty (pre-Argon2id validation)
- Call acceptInvitation(pool, token, password, name, cookieSecret)
- ValidationError → 400; InvalidLinkError → 410; AccountAlreadyExistsError → 409
- On success: res.setHeader('Cache-Control','no-store'); res.json({ email })

## serverApp.ts mounting order (CRITICAL — plan.md §Edge Cases)
- Anonymous /api/auth/invitation/* routes registered BEFORE app.all('/api/auth/*', toNodeHandler)
- Admin /api/admin/invitations routes are already after requireAdmin gate

## Acceptance
- `npx jest src/server/controllers/invitationController.test.ts --runInBand` passes (all GREEN)
- `yarn typecheck` passes

description:

**Plan**: plan.md §Project Structure (invitationStore.ts), data-model.md §Management-list query (JOIN, invitedByEmail, no tokenHash/tokenEnc in select, Cache-Control), data-model.md §State machine (retract conditional UPDATE — plan.md Pass 11), §tokenEnc encryption rules (re-copy decrypt)

## Test cases for listInvitations(pool)

2. Returns an empty array when no invitations exist (FR-013 empty state)
3. Orders newest first (createdAt DESC)
4. Does NOT include tokenHash or tokenEnc in the returned objects (data-model.md — secrets never reach the response)
5. Lazy-expire: a pending row with past expiresAt appears as status 'expired' (lazy UPDATE runs on list load)
6. For an Accepted invitation: acceptedAt is present; for others: acceptedAt is null

## Test cases for retractInvitation(pool, id)
1. Pending invitation → status becomes 'retracted', returns InvitationSummary with invitedByEmail (FR-015)
2. Non-existent id → throws NotFoundError (→ 404)
3. Already-accepted invitation → throws NotPendingError (→ 409)
4. Already-retracted invitation → throws NotPendingError (→ 409)
5. RACE — accept races retract on same pending invite: the accept commits first (status='accepted'), then retract must get 0 rows from conditional UPDATE → throws NotPendingError, does NOT overwrite the accepted state (plan.md Pass 11 TOCTOU test)

## Test cases for getInvitationLink(pool, id, cookieSecret)
1. Pending invitation → decrypts tokenEnc → returns { link: buildInvitationLink(decryptedToken, baseUrl) } — same original link (FR-016)
2. Non-existent id → throws NotFoundError (→ 404)
3. Non-pending invitation → throws NotPendingError (→ 409)
4. tokenEnc decrypt failure (wrong cookieSecret) → throws DecryptError (→ 409 'link unavailable', not 500, per data-model.md)

## Acceptance
- `npx jest src/server/auth/invitationStore.test.ts --runInBand` shows new tests failing (RED)
close_reason: added failing tests for listInvitations, retractInvitation, getInvitationLink with stub exports in invitationStore.ts

title: US3 [GREEN]: Implement invitationStore list + retract + recopy paths
description:
**Spec**: specs/002-invitation-system/spec.md §FR-013..FR-019
**Plan**: plan.md §Project Structure, data-model.md §Management-list query, §State machine (retract conditional UPDATE), §tokenEnc encryption rules (re-copy), plan.md Pass 11 (retract TOCTOU fix, retract invitedByEmail JOIN)
**Skills**: /typescript-unit-testing
**Files**: src/server/auth/invitationStore.ts (EDIT — add listInvitations, retractInvitation, getInvitationLink)

## Task
Add three new functions to invitationStore.ts to make the US3 RED tests pass.

## listInvitations(pool: Pool): Promise<InvitationSummary[]>
- Lazy-expire first: UPDATE invitation SET status='expired' WHERE status='pending' AND expiresAt <= now()
- SELECT invitation.id, email, role, status, createdAt, expiresAt, acceptedAt, user.email AS invitedByEmail
  FROM invitation
  INNER JOIN "user" ON "user".id = invitation."invitedBy"
  ORDER BY invitation."createdAt" DESC
- MUST NOT select tokenHash or tokenEnc (secrets never reach response)
- Return the array (empty if none exist — not an error)
- Set Cache-Control: no-store on the HTTP response (controller does this; store returns data)

## retractInvitation(pool: Pool, id: string): Promise<InvitationSummary>
- ATOMIC conditional UPDATE (plan.md Pass 11 — NOT a SELECT-then-UPDATE):
  UPDATE invitation SET status='retracted' WHERE id=$1 AND status='pending' RETURNING id
- If 0 rows updated:
  - SELECT 1 FROM invitation WHERE id=$1 → if no row: throw NotFoundError (→ 404)
  - if row exists: throw NotPendingError (→ 409) — invitation is not pending
- If 1 row: fetch and return the full InvitationSummary (with invitedByEmail via JOIN to user — same mapper as list)
- Set Cache-Control: no-store on the HTTP response (controller does this)

## getInvitationLink(pool: Pool, id: string, baseUrl: string, cookieSecret: string): Promise<{ link: string }>
- SELECT id, status, tokenEnc FROM invitation WHERE id=$1
- If not found: throw NotFoundError
- If status \!= 'pending': throw NotPendingError
- Decrypt: decryptToken(tokenEnc, cookieSecret) → plaintext token or null
- If null (decrypt failure after cookieSecret rotation): throw DecryptError (→ 409 'link unavailable', not 500)
- Return { link: buildInvitationLink(token, baseUrl) }

## New error classes to export
- NotFoundError (→ 404)
- NotPendingError (→ 409)
- DecryptError (→ 409 'link unavailable')

## Shared row-mapper
- Factor InvitationSummary assembly (invitation columns + user.email JOIN) into a shared private helper so list and retract cannot diverge (plan.md Pass 11 congruence)

## Acceptance
- `npx jest src/server/auth/invitationStore.test.ts --runInBand` passes (all tests GREEN)
- `yarn typecheck` passes
close_reason: implemented listInvitations, retractInvitation, getInvitationLink in invitationStore.ts; fixed test call sites for getInvitationLink to pass baseUrl; typecheck passes

title: US3 [GREEN]: Implement admin list + retract + recopy controller routes
description:
**Spec**: specs/002-invitation-system/spec.md §FR-013..FR-019
**Plan**: plan.md §Security Considerations (Pass 4 CSRF, Pass 10 list Cache-Control, Pass 11 retract Cache-Control + invitedByEmail), contracts/invitation-api.yaml §admin routes
**Skills**: /worker-request-handler
**Files**: src/server/controllers/invitationController.ts (EDIT — add admin list/retract/recopy handlers)

## Task
Add the three admin management routes to invitationController.ts.

## GET /api/admin/invitations handler (list)
- No CSRF check needed (read-only GET — plan.md Pass 4 note)
- Call listInvitations(pool)
- Set Cache-Control: no-store; Pragma: no-cache (plan.md Pass 10 — largest PII surface)
- Return array (empty array is OK, not an error per FR-013)

## POST /api/admin/invitations/:id/retract handler
- Apply CSRF middleware (shared helper from US1 controller — Pass 4)
- Call retractInvitation(pool, id)
- NotFoundError → 404
- NotPendingError → 409
- On success: set Cache-Control: no-store; Pragma: no-cache (plan.md Pass 11); return InvitationSummary

## GET /api/admin/invitations/:id/link handler (re-copy)
- No CSRF check (read-only GET, returns secret — but same-origin fetch posture prevents cross-origin body read; plan.md Pass 4)
- Call getInvitationLink(pool, id, baseUrl, cookieSecret)
- NotFoundError → 404
- NotPendingError → 409 'invitation is not pending'
- DecryptError → 409 'link unavailable' (cookieSecret rotation degraded recopy, plan.md Pass 1 / data-model.md)
- On success: set Cache-Control: no-store; Pragma: no-cache (plan.md Pass 7); return { link }

## Cache-Control summary (all email/link-bearing responses must be no-store — plan.md Pass 11)
The complete set: create 201, list 200, retract 200, re-copy link 200, anonymous lookup 200, accept 200 — all no-store.

title: Final: Integration test — full create → redeem → retract flow
description:
**Spec**: specs/002-invitation-system/spec.md §SC-001..SC-009, §Edge Cases
**Plan**: plan.md §Project Structure (invitation.integration.test.ts), research.md §Cross-cutting: Testing strategy
**Skills**: /vitest-integration-testing
**Files**: src/server/auth/invitation.integration.test.ts (CREATE NEW)

## Context
Integration tests run against the real compiled server (better-auth is ESM-only, hence the separate test harness). Use the existing invitation.integration.test.ts pattern established by auth.integration.test.ts.

## Why integration tests (in addition to unit tests)
Unit tests (invitationStore.test.ts, invitationController.test.ts) run under Jest/CommonJS and mock or stub better-auth-related behavior. The cross-layer behavior that requires a real running server:
- A newly created account via acceptInvitation() can actually sign in with better-auth (FR-012 end-to-end, plan.md Pass 8 latent coupling)
- Route ordering: anonymous invitation routes registered BEFORE the better-auth catch-all (plan.md §Edge Cases Route ordering)
- CSRF + rate-limit enforcement with BETTER_AUTH_ENFORCE_ORIGIN=1 + BETTER_AUTH_ENFORCE_RATE_LIMIT=1

## Scenarios to cover
1. Full create → redeem flow: admin creates invitation → recipient GETs token → recipient POSTs accept → account created with correct role → recipient signs in (FR-012 — better-auth sign-in on the created account works)
2. Single-use: reusing the accepted link → 410 (SC-003)
3. Retract: admin creates → admin retracts → recipient GETs token → 410 (link stopped working, SC-004)
4. Expiry: create an invitation with a past expiresAt (insert directly) → link returns 410
5. Rate limit: 11 rapid requests to GET /api/auth/invitation/:token → 429 (BETTER_AUTH_ENFORCE_RATE_LIMIT=1)
6. CSRF: POST /api/auth/invitation/accept with wrong Origin → 403 (BETTER_AUTH_ENFORCE_ORIGIN=1)
7. CSRF on admin POST: POST /api/admin/invitations with wrong Origin → 403
8. Body parsing: malformed JSON to POST /api/auth/invitation/accept → JSON 400 (not HTML)
9. Re-copy: admin creates → admin re-copies link → same link (FR-016)
10. Empty management list: GET /api/admin/invitations → [] (not error)
11. Log redaction: GET /invitation/<token> in production-like config → logger emits placeholder not raw token (unit-level test of the logger redaction helper, per plan.md Pass 7 note — or assert directly on the logger middleware unit)

## Acceptance
- `yarn test:integration` passes for the new invitation integration tests
- The full create → redeem → sign-in flow is exercised end-to-end

**Added by sp:06-analyze (skill-ref fix):** The skill reference '/vitest-integration-testing' does not exist in this project. Use the existing integration test pattern from src/server/auth/auth.integration.test.ts as reference. The implementation guidance above is correct.

title: Final: Design quality pass — microcopy, empty-state, and non-leaky error messages
description:
**Spec**: specs/002-invitation-system/spec.md §FR-010, §SC-002
**Plan**: plan.md §Presentation Design §Quality Pass (/design-clarify for invalid-link and validation microcopy; /design-clarify for empty-state copy on management list)
**Skills**: /design-clarify
**Files**: Review all invitation UI components after implementation:
- src/frontend/web/invitations/CreateInvitation.tsx
- src/frontend/web/auth/RedeemInvitation.tsx
- src/frontend/web/invitations/InvitationsList.tsx
- src/core/i18n/locales/en.ts (string values)

## Quality pass goals (plan.md §Quality Pass)
**Design quality target**: MVP (internal admin tool; correctness and clarity over polish).
**What to review**:
1. Invalid-link error messages (RedeemInvitation): must be non-leaky (FR-010) — does not reveal which of unknown/accepted/retracted/expired applies; must be clear enough that the recipient knows what to do (contact the admin for a new invite)
2. Validation microcopy (CreateInvitation): error messages for malformed email, bad role, account-exists 409, active-pending 409 — are these clear and actionable for an admin?
3. Empty-state copy (InvitationsList): when no invitations exist, the message should orient the admin ('No invitations yet. Create one above.')
4. 429 rate-limit copy (RedeemInvitation): must clearly distinguish from the invalid-link terminal message — should invite a retry, not imply the link is dead (plan.md Pass 9)
5. Accessibility microcopy: copy-success announcement for the copy-link button must be announced clearly (not just 'Copied' but something descriptive like 'Invitation link copied to clipboard')

## What to do
- Review all string values in en.ts for the above criteria
- Refine any copy that is ambiguous, leaky, or fails the clarity bar
- Verify the 429 and 410 messages are DISTINCT from each other in both lookup and accept forms

## Acceptance
- All invalid-link messages are non-leaky (do not reveal which terminal state applies)
- 429 message is clearly transient and invites a retry
- Empty-state text is descriptive and action-oriented
- Copy-success live-region text is descriptive
- `yarn typecheck` still passes after any string-value changes

title: Remediate: invitationRateLimit prune does a full-table scan per request (no index on lastRequest)
description:
**Review**: [sp:security-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: migrations/1781767466145-AddInvitationRateLimitTable.js, src/server/controllers/invitationController.ts

**Finding** (MINOR / availability):
invitationRateLimit() runs `DELETE FROM "invitationRateLimit" WHERE "lastRequest" < $1` on every anonymous invitation request (invitationController.ts ~line 169), but the invitationRateLimit table has no index on "lastRequest" — only the "key" PRIMARY KEY. Each prune is therefore a sequential scan. The plan (plan.md lines 384-386) assumes this prune is "cheap, bounded" but did not add the supporting index. Combined with the already-accepted X-Forwarded-For spoofing risk (each distinct spoofed IP key inserts a row), an attacker who inflates the table turns every subsequent /api/auth/invitation/* request into a growing full-table scan — a mild DoS amplifier on the unauthenticated surface.

**Fix suggestion**:
- Add a btree index on "lastRequest" in the migration (or a follow-up migration):
  CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS "idx_invitationRateLimit_lastRequest" ON "invitationRateLimit"("lastRequest");
- Alternatively prune probabilistically (e.g. only ~1/N requests) so the scan cost is amortized, or prune only the current key's stale window.

**Acceptance**:
- A migration adds an index on invitationRateLimit("lastRequest") (or the prune no longer scans the whole table on every request).
- EXPLAIN on the prune DELETE uses the index (Index Scan, not Seq Scan) when the table has >1 row.
- Existing invitation rate-limit integration tests still pass.
close_reason: Added migration 1781767466146 with btree index on invitationRateLimit(lastRequest) to eliminate full-table scan during prune DELETE

title: Remediate: 409 error discrimination in invitationThunks never matches real server responses
description:
**Review**: [sp:architecture-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/frontend/web/invitations/invitationThunks.ts, src/server/controllers/invitationController.ts

**Finding**:
invitationThunks.ts lines 58-65 branch on 409 responses by checking `errorText.startsWith('account_exists')` and `errorText.startsWith('active_pending')`. The real server returns `err.message` which is `'An account already exists for <email>'` (AccountExistsError) and `'An active pending invitation already exists for <email>'` (ActivePendingError). Neither message starts with those prefixes, so every 409 falls through to the `'validation_error'` branch regardless of the actual cause. The unit test mocks the response body as `{ error: 'account_exists: ...' }` so it passes, but production responses never match.

**Fix suggestion**:
- Add a machine-readable discriminator to the 409 responses in invitationController.ts: return `{ error: err.message, code: err.code }` (err.code is 'ACCOUNT_EXISTS' or 'PENDING_INVITE_EXISTS').
- In invitationThunks.ts, branch on `body.code` rather than `errorText.startsWith(...)`.
- Update the unit test mocks to match the new server response shape.
- Verify with the integration test that the 409 body includes `code`.

**Acceptance**:
- POST /api/admin/invitations with an existing account email returns JSON `{ error: '...', code: 'ACCOUNT_EXISTS' }`.
- POST /api/admin/invitations for an already-pending email returns JSON `{ error: '...', code: 'PENDING_INVITE_EXISTS' }`.
- invitationThunks.ts branches correctly on the `code` field, not on message prefix.
- All existing invitationThunks unit tests updated and passing.
- Integration tests confirm the 409 body shape.
close_reason: Added code field to 409 responses in invitationController; invitationThunks now branches on body.code (ACCOUNT_EXISTS/PENDING_INVITE_EXISTS); tests updated to match real server shape

title: Remediate: invitation API routes absent from core/interfaces/Api.ts typed contract
description:
**Review**: [sp:architecture-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/core/interfaces/Api.ts, src/frontend/web/invitations/invitationThunks.ts, src/frontend/web/invitations/invitationsListThunks.ts, src/frontend/web/auth/redeemInvitationThunks.ts

**Finding**:
All invitation thunks (`invitationThunks.ts`, `invitationsListThunks.ts`, `redeemInvitationThunks.ts`) call the server via raw `fetch()` with untyped URL strings and manually-cast `response.json()`. Every other web-only route in this project (`/api/auth/get-session`, `/api/admin/languages`, etc.) is registered in `src/core/interfaces/Api.ts` with typed request params and response shapes, then accessed through the typed `RequestContext` API client. The invitation surface bypasses this contract layer, losing compile-time type checking on API call sites.

**Fix suggestion**:
- Add invitation route entries to the `APIGet` and `APIPost` interfaces in `src/core/interfaces/Api.ts` (under '// Web Only') with typed param and response shapes.
- Refactor the invitation thunks to use the typed RequestContext/useLoad API client, or at minimum import the response types from the core interface so the `as SomeType` casts are grounded in a shared definition.
- Note: the routes involve path params (`/api/auth/invitation/:token`, `/api/admin/invitations/:id/retract`) which may require new param types in the typed interface.

**Acceptance**:
- All invitation-related GET/POST routes appear in `src/core/interfaces/Api.ts`.
- `tsc --noEmit` across all projects still passes with no new errors.
- Invitation thunks no longer cast raw `response.json()` to ungrounded types.
close_reason: Work complete: invitation routes added to core Api.ts typed contract (6916cda95e06a33d95cc59c8beec2a2a451097a6), thunks grounded in shared types. Worker returned prose without br close; orchestrator closed after confirming HEAD advanced. [overnight autonomous recovery]

title: Remediate: assembly download UI gets stuck on transient download failure
description:
**Review**: [sp:code-quality-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/frontend/web/documents/useAssembleQuarter.tsx

**Finding**:
In the `poll()` callback (lines 82-101), the `ready` branch calls `stopPolling()` and then `await downloadAndFinish()` INSIDE poll's own try/catch, whose catch block silently swallows all errors (intended only for transient poll-request failures, per the comment at lines 83-85). If `downloadAndFinish()`'s `Axios.get(.../download, {responseType:"blob"})` fails (network drop, server 404 "result has expired", etc.), the error is swallowed, but polling has ALREADY been stopped. The hook's `status` state is left at `{tag:"running"}` (or whatever it was before the ready poll) forever — the UI shows an indefinite "Assembling…" state with no error message, no retry, and no way to recover short of a page reload.

**Fix suggestion**:
Move the `stopPolling()` + `downloadAndFinish()` call out of the shared try/catch (or give it its own try/catch) so a download failure sets `status` to `{tag:"failed", reason: ...}` instead of being swallowed by the generic transient-poll-failure handler.

**Acceptance**:
- A test simulates `downloadAndFinish()` throwing (e.g. the download GET rejects) after a `ready` poll response, and asserts the hook's status transitions to `failed` with a user-visible reason (not stuck at `running`/`queued`).
close_reason: Moved downloadAndFinish() into its own try/catch in poll()'s ready branch; added test for post-ready download failure -> failed status

title: Repo hygiene: remove disposable spike output binaries (25MB) from git history-forward
description:
**Review**: [sp:code-quality-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: specs/007-assembled-quarter-download/spike/out/*, specs/007-assembled-quarter-download/spike/odm/out/*

**Finding**:
The branch commits ~25MB of disposable spike-proof-of-concept output binaries directly into the repo: `spike/out/Luke-2-assembled.{odt,pdf}`, `spike/out/Luke-2-oddpage.{odt,pdf}`, `spike/odm/out/Luke-2-odm.{odt,odm,pdf}`, `spike/odm/out/Chris-Q2-rewired.{odt,odm,pdf}`. These are throwaway artifacts from the WS-2a/WS-2a' feasibility spikes (already closed tasks lessons-from-luke-koog.1/.8) — they aren't referenced by any test or build step and only bloat the repo/clone size going forward. (Distinct from `test/docs/references/English_Luke-Q2-Master-bilingual.odt`, which IS a live test fixture and should stay.)

**Fix suggestion**:
Delete the `spike/*/out/` directories (or move the spike findings' PDFs to an external location and keep only `FINDINGS.md`) in a follow-up commit. If historical repo size is a concern, this can be a `git rm` without full history rewrite (rewriting history is likely disproportionate for one branch's spike).

**Acceptance**:
- `specs/007-assembled-quarter-download/spike/out/` and `specs/007-assembled-quarter-download/spike/odm/out/` no longer contain generated `.odt`/`.odm`/`.pdf` binaries on the default branch.
- `FINDINGS.md`/`FINDINGS-odm.md` (the actual spike conclusions) are retained.
close_reason: Removed 25MB disposable spike output binaries (git rm); added .gitignore rule; FINDINGS.md/FINDINGS-odm.md retained

title: Remediate: assembly job failure reason can leak raw error text to unauthenticated client
description:
**Review**: [sp:security-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/assembly/AssemblyJobRegistry.ts, src/server/actions/assembleQuarter.ts, src/server/controllers/assemblyController.ts

**Finding** (MINOR, info-disclosure):
AssemblyJobRegistry.ts:296-298 forwards a failed job's raw `error.message` verbatim as the client-facing `reason`. That reason is returned by BOTH status endpoints, including the unauthenticated key-based route GET /api/languages/:id/quarters/:book/:series/assembly?mode= (no UUID needed — reachable by anyone who knows language/book/series). The runner's curated fixed-vocabulary catches are NOT exhaustive, so an operational fault can throw a raw message (absolute filesystem path or DB error text) that escapes to the client. Uncovered throw sites:
- assembleQuarter.ts:116 fs.copyFileSync — sits BETWEEN the two try blocks, unguarded
- assembleQuarter.ts:98 mkdirSafe(jobDir) — unguarded
- makeRunner storage calls (assemblyController.ts ~119-131: storage.language/lessons/lesson) — unguarded

**Fix suggestion**:
Curate at the RUNNER BOUNDARY so every throw out of the runner is a fixed-vocabulary, path-free message (wrap the makeRunner body / assembleQuarter's copy+storage+mkdir steps). Do NOT add a blanket-generic catch at the registry — the registry legitimately forwards useful curated reasons (e.g. 'missing constituent: …', 'a lesson failed to generate: …'); a generic registry catch would regress those.

**Acceptance**:
- A copyFileSync / mkdirSafe / storage-layer failure during assembly surfaces a fixed-vocabulary reason with no absolute path, stack trace, or DB error text.
- Existing curated reasons (missing constituent, lesson failed to generate/prepare, timeout, produced no result) still pass through unchanged.
- A test asserts the runner never rejects with a raw fs/DB error message.
close_reason: Curated mkdirSafe/copyFileSync/storage-layer errors at the runner boundary to fixed-vocabulary reasons; added tests asserting raw fs/DB errors never leak.

title: Remediate: literal NUL bytes embedded in AssemblyJobRegistry.ts source
description:
**Review**: [sp:code-quality-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/assembly/AssemblyJobRegistry.ts

**Finding**:
The private `toKeyString()` method builds the dedup key with literal NUL (0x00) control-byte separators embedded directly in the template literal source (around line 196: the key string interpolates languageId/book/series/mode). This is not an editor-rendered escape sequence -- the committed file contains actual NUL bytes, which makes `git diff`/`git show`/GitHub treat the entire file as binary ("Binary files differ"), breaking line-level diff, blame, and review tooling for this file going forward. It also risks mangling in tools that assume text encoding (some linters/editors mishandle embedded NULs).

**Fix suggestion**:
Replace the NUL-byte separator with a plain printable delimiter that cannot collide with any field value (e.g. "|", since book and mode are fixed enums and languageId/series are integers), or build the key via JSON.stringify([key.languageId, key.book, key.series, key.mode]). Re-save the file as normal UTF-8 text so git diff renders it as text again.

**Acceptance**:
- git diff / git show on AssemblyJobRegistry.ts renders as text, not "Binary files differ"
- No NUL bytes remain in the file
- Existing AssemblyJobRegistry.test.ts suite (dedup/key-identity tests) still passes unchanged
close_reason: Replaced NUL-byte dedup key separator with a pipe delimiter; file is now clean UTF-8 text with no NULs, tests pass unchanged

title: Remediate: duplicated docsDirPath env-branch logic in serverApp.ts
description:
**Review**: [sp:architecture-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/serverApp.ts, src/server/storage/docStorage.ts

**Finding**:
src/server/serverApp.ts's new assemblyWorkRoot() (added for the assembled-quarter-download feature) re-implements docStorage.ts's private docsDirPath() env-branching (test/development/production subpath selection) verbatim instead of reusing it. docsDirPath() is not exported from docStorage.ts's default export object, so serverApp.ts had no way to reuse it and duplicated the logic inline. This creates two independent sources of truth for the per-env docs root; a future change to one (e.g. adding a new NODE_ENV branch) can silently drift from the other.

**Fix suggestion**:
Export docsDirPath (or an equivalent helper) from docStorage.ts's default export, and have serverApp.ts's assemblyWorkRoot() call it (joining the '/assembly-work' subpath) instead of re-deriving the env branch itself.

**Acceptance**:
- Only one function computes the per-env docs root subpath.
- serverApp.ts's assemblyWorkRoot() delegates to docStorage's shared helper.
- Existing tests (docStorage tests, serverApp/assemblyController tests) still pass.
close_reason: Exported docsDirPath from docStorage.ts default export; assemblyWorkRoot() in serverApp.ts now delegates to it instead of duplicating the NODE_ENV branch

title: Remediate: rezipWithMimetypeFirst duplicated verbatim across flattenFooterFields.ts and renameMasterPageStyles.ts
description:
**Review**: [sp:code-quality-review] cycle 3
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/actions/flattenFooterFields.ts, src/server/actions/renameMasterPageStyles.ts

**Finding**:
`rezipWithMimetypeFirst` (re-zip an extracted ODT dir with the ODF-required mimetype-stored-first-and-uncompressed ordering) is byte-identical in both files (each ~9 lines, same execFileSync recipe). A future change to the ODF re-pack recipe (e.g. compression flags, error handling) has to be made twice and can silently drift.

**Fix suggestion**:
Extract `rezipWithMimetypeFirst` into a shared module (e.g. `src/server/xml/rezipWithMimetypeFirst.ts` alongside the existing `mergeXml.ts`/`extractNamespaces` helpers) and import it from both `flattenFooterFields.ts` and `renameMasterPageStyles.ts`.

**Acceptance**:
- Single implementation of `rezipWithMimetypeFirst`, imported by both callers
- Existing `flattenFooterFields.test.ts` / `renameMasterPageStyles.test.ts` still pass unchanged (behavior-preserving refactor)
close_reason: Extracted shared rezipWithMimetypeFirst helper into src/server/xml/rezipWithMimetypeFirst.ts, imported by both callers

title: AssemblyJobRegistry: dedup, lifecycle, timeout, cap, eviction (RED)
description:
**Spec**: specs/007-assembled-quarter-download/data-model.md §'AssemblyJobRegistry (in-memory, server-only)' and §'AssemblyJob' state transitions, specs/007-assembled-quarter-download/research.md §R5, plan.md Red-Team Passes 1/2/3/5
**Skills**: test-driven-development, prefactoring
**Files**: src/server/assembly/AssemblyJobRegistry.ts (NEW), src/server/assembly/AssemblyJobRegistry.test.ts (NEW)
**CRITICAL context a fresh implementer needs (do not skip)**:
- **Synchronous dedup (FR-010, Pass 1/2)**: `startOrAttach(key)` MUST check-then-insert a `queued` placeholder **synchronously, before any `await`** — otherwise two concurrent starts both see 'no job' and both run full assembly. Node's single-threaded loop makes a purely sync check-then-insert atomic.
- **Dedup-before-cap ordering (Pass 2 finding A)**: critical section order is strictly dedup-check → (on miss) cap-check → insert. An attach to an existing live job returns unconditionally and is NEVER rejected by the queue-depth cap; only a genuinely new key can be capped.
- **Terminal-attach / retry rule (Pass 5, FR-009)**: attach applies to `queued`/`running`/valid-`ready` (result file present). A `failed` entry, or a `ready` entry whose result file is gone, is treated as ABSENT — `startOrAttach` evicts it and starts a fresh job (new jobId, `queued`). Getting this wrong makes retry-after-failure impossible (FR-009 violation).
- **Timeout clock starts at run-start (slot acquisition), not enqueue (Pass 2 finding D)** — a queued job waiting behind others must never be spuriously timed out before it runs.
- **Queue-depth cap**: max live (`queued`+`running`) jobs; exceeding it on a genuinely-new key is surfaced by the caller as 429 (registry itself just needs to expose a capacity check/rejection).
- **Terminal-entry eviction**: `ready`/`failed` entries evicted on a TTL aligned with the existing `docStorage` 24h cleanup window, and a `ready` entry is dropped as soon as its result file is found missing.
- **Concurrency-1 for the soffice step**: registry serializes the actual merge invocation (a single-slot queue), independent of how many keys are queued.
Write RED unit tests (soffice mocked/stubbed — this is pure job-model logic) for: dedup/attach on live job, dedup-before-cap ordering, terminal-attach eviction (failed → fresh job, pruned-ready → fresh job, valid-ready → attach), concurrency-1 serialization ordering, timeout-from-run-start, cap rejection, TTL/pruned-file eviction.
**Acceptance**: tests fail for the right reason; run via `npx jest src/server/assembly/AssemblyJobRegistry.test.ts --runInBand`.

**Type**: RED
close_reason: RED: added failing AssemblyJobRegistry unit tests (dedup/attach, dedup-before-cap ordering, terminal-attach retry, concurrency-1, run-start timeout, cap rejection, TTL eviction) with a not-implemented stub; genuine assertion-level red confirmed via jest

title: Red: flattenFooterFields escape + fallback + safe re-zip
description:
**Type**: RED
**Spec**: specs/007-assembled-quarter-download/research.md §R4, specs/007-assembled-quarter-download/data-model.md §'Validation rules summary' (flattened footer row), plan.md Red-Team 'Security & Robustness'
**Skills**: test-driven-development
**Files**: src/server/actions/flattenFooterFields.ts (NEW), src/server/actions/flattenFooterFields.test.ts (NEW), plus a small fixture ODT/styles.xml under a test fixtures dir
**Context a fresh implementer needs**:
- Reads each constituent ODT's `meta.xml` custom properties (`Quarter`, `Lesson`) and substitutes the `<text:user-defined name="Quarter"/"Lesson">…</text:user-defined>` elements in `styles.xml` (footer lives there) with literal resolved values, then re-zips.
- **XML-escape every substituted value** (`& < >`) — a raw value with metacharacters produces malformed `styles.xml` that fails to open.
- **Missing/empty property fallback**: if `Quarter`/`Lesson` custom property is absent, derive the value from the lesson's own `series`/`lesson` fields rather than emitting a blank field.
- **Re-zip sharp edge (research.md R4)**: ODF requires the `mimetype` entry stored FIRST and UNCOMPRESSED. `fsUtils.zip`'s `zip -r` does NOT guarantee this ordering — do not reuse it blindly; write a dedicated ODF-safe repack (mimetype stored-first, rest deflated) or explicitly reorder.
- Operates on a COPY (see the assembleQuarter task for why — this function itself is provenance-agnostic, it's the caller's job to pass a safe path).
Write RED tests: metacharacter-bearing values escape correctly; missing/empty property falls back to derived series/lesson; repacked ODT opens (mimetype-first, uncompressed) and the field reads as literal text (not a field reference).
**Acceptance**: tests fail for the right reason; `npx jest src/server/actions/flattenFooterFields.test.ts --runInBand`.

title: Red: sofficeAssemble warm/inject/run + process-group kill
description:
**Spec**: specs/007-assembled-quarter-download/research.md §R1, §R5, specs/007-assembled-quarter-download/data-model.md §AssemblyJobRegistry (working-dir lifecycle), plan.md Red-Team Pass 1 ('Killing a hung soffice') and Pass 3 ('Temp-Dir Lifecycle')
**Skills**: test-driven-development
**Files**: src/server/assembly/sofficeAssemble.ts (NEW), src/server/assembly/sofficeAssemble.test.ts (NEW, unit — soffice invocation mocked/stubbed via child_process spy), src/server/assembly/macro/Module1.xba (NEW, ported from spike/macro-template/basic/Standard/Module1.xba)
**Context a fresh implementer needs**:
- Three-step flow (spike-proven): (1) warm a fresh mktemp profile (`soffice --headless --convert-to odt` on a throwaway file) so LO builds `user/basic`; (2) inject `macro/Module1.xba` into `<profile>/user/basic/Standard/`, `rm -f <profile>/.lock`; (3) run `soffice --headless --norestore --nologo -env:UserInstallation=file://<profile> macro:///Standard.Module1.Assemble`, passing the ordered ASCII file list (`00.odt`..`13.odt`, see assembleQuarter task) + output URL via env vars.
- Macro inserts `PARAGRAPH_BREAK` + `BreakType=PAGE_BEFORE` before each doc 2..14, calls `insertDocumentFromURL(url, {UpdateDocMode: NO_UPDATE})`, then `storeToURL(FilterName="writer8")`.
- **Process-group kill (Pass 1)**: soffice launches via oosplash which forks soffice.bin; a plain PID kill can orphan soffice.bin. Spawn in its own process group (`detached: true`) and kill the group (`process.kill(-pid, ...)`) on timeout, so no orphan survives a forced kill.
- **Per-job working-dir root (Pass 3)**: NOT a bare `mktemp -d` — use `<docStorage>/assembly-work/<jobId>/` (a single known dedicated root) so it can be swept on startup (see the registry-init sweep, wired in the assembleQuarter/controller task). `rm -rf` eagerly on completion/caught-crash.
- **Result verification (Pass 2 finding F)**: after the run, caller must `stat` the result path and require non-zero size + valid ODT mimetype-first entry before treating it as success — this wrapper should return enough info (or the caller does the stat) to support that; do not silently trust a zero-return-code as success.
- Hard timeout (~2-3x the ~40s observed baseline) that kills the process group and returns/throws a distinguishable timeout error.
Write RED unit tests (mock child_process.spawn) for: correct arg construction, process-group spawn options, timeout triggers a group kill not a lone-PID kill, profile path derivation is per-job.
**Acceptance**: tests fail for the right reason; `npx jest src/server/assembly/sofficeAssemble.test.ts --runInBand`.

**Type**: RED
close_reason: RED: sofficeAssemble stub + failing tests for arg construction, detached process-group spawn, timeout group-kill, per-job profile derivation

title: assembly-work root: startup sweep of orphaned per-job working dirs
description:
**Spec**: specs/007-assembled-quarter-download/data-model.md §AssemblyJobRegistry ('Working dirs live under a single known dedicated root...swept on startup'), plan.md Red-Team Pass 3 'Temp-Dir Lifecycle' (MEDIUM)
**Skills**: test-driven-development
**Files**: src/server/assembly/AssemblyJobRegistry.ts (init sweep) or a small src/server/assembly/sweepAssemblyWork.ts (NEW) + test
**Context**: abrupt process death (SIGKILL/OOM, and `custom:restart_passenger` which fires on EVERY Capistrano deploy) skips the eager rm -rf cleanup, so `<docStorage>/assembly-work/<jobId>/` dirs can orphan. On registry init (server startup), sweep the whole `assembly-work/` root and remove any leftover per-job dirs — this is the ONLY cleanup path for abrupt-death orphans. This is safe ONLY under the single-process deployment constraint (see the separate Passenger single-process-pin operational task) — do not run this sweep logic assuming multi-worker safety.
**Acceptance**: unit test asserts sweep removes pre-existing subdirs under assembly-work/ on init and does not throw if the root doesn't exist yet; `yarn typecheck`; `yarn lint` clean.
close_reason: Added sweepAssemblyWork.ts: removes orphaned per-job dirs under assembly-work/ on init, no-op if root missing; unit-tested

comments.author: eykd
comments.text:
ROOT CAUSE (orchestrator diagnosis): Two issues in .claude/skills/ralph/commit-red.ts, shared cross-feature tooling.
(1) SYMPTOM: isTestFile regex is /\.(spec|test)\.ts$/ — does NOT match .tsx, so a frontend RED task's staged *.test.tsx files aren't recognized; gate 3 refuses 'no staged test file'.
(2) ROOT: gate 4 runs 'npx vitest run', but this is a JEST project — vitest is NOT installed (not in devDependencies, no node_modules/.bin/vitest). execFileSync errors -> caught -> returns false='failing', so gate 4 verifies nothing (inert for ALL prior .ts RED commits too). Widening the regex alone unblocks commits through a gate that checks nothing.
VERIFIED: staged .test.tsx tests genuinely FAIL under jest (7 failed/2 passed via 'npx jest useAssembleQuarter.test.tsx AssembleQuarterButton.test.tsx --runInBand') — valid RED state; staged impl files are stubs. Lint-staged already honors RALPH_RED_COMMIT sentinel to skip jest for .tsx, so no wrapper change needed.
STATE: 4 files staged in index (2 tests + 2 stub impls); .beads/issues.jsonl unstaged. Drain paused pending user decision on the tooling fix.

title: US1: golden-reference integration test — pagination/footer parity + source byte-identity guard
description:
**Spec**: specs/007-assembled-quarter-download/plan.md §'Acceptance Test Strategy' 'Golden-reference check', plan.md Red-Team Pass 6 (MEDIUM — source-immutability assertion), Pass 5 (CRITICAL context)
**Skills**: test-driven-development
**Files**: src/server/actions/assembleQuarter.integration.test.ts (NEW, opt-in via `yarn test:integration`, serialized — real soffice), test fixtures (see the fixtures task below for sourcing the reference .odt)
**Context a fresh implementer MUST read**:
- Assembles the REAL series-2 English-bilingual quarter (the 14 masters already in test/docs/serverDocs/, per quickstart.md) against the committed reference `English_Luke-Q2-Master-bilingual.odt` (or Q1 if that's the fixture actually committed — confirm against whichever the fixtures task lands).
- Assertions (functional/visual equivalence via PDF render + pdftotext, NOT byte-for-byte ODT diff — LO-merge and manual-export never produce clean diffs): (1) content + lesson ordering (TOC then 1-13); (2) continuous page numbering, never resets per lesson; (3) each lesson's first page suppresses its number; (4) footer Quarter/Lesson fields populated (not blank); (5) page-number sequence matches the reference including its +1 offset (page = physical+1 is EXPECTED, not a bug — do not 'fix' it).
- **MANDATORY source-immutability guard (Pass 6)**: this is precisely the English-bilingual case where `makeLessonFile` returns the raw `docs/`-or-`test/docs/serverDocs/` SOURCE file (Pass 5 CRITICAL). Before assembly, SHA-256-hash each of the 14 source constituent .odt files on disk; after assembly completes, re-hash and assert byte-identity for every one. Fail loudly (not silently) if any source file changed. This is the ONLY thing that would catch a regression to in-place mutation in assembleQuarter/flattenFooterFields — the pagination/footer assertions above are all computed on the produced book and would pass green even if the source were corrupted.
- Close LibreOffice Writer before running (quickstart.md gotcha); run foreground/attached on macOS.
**Acceptance**: `yarn test:integration` passes locally with soffice + fixtures present; test fails loudly (not silently) if the source-hash assertion is violated (verify by temporarily breaking copy-before-flatten and confirming this test — not just parity — catches it, then revert).
close_reason: grouping node complete (all child tasks closed)

title: Fix: per-lesson footer Quarter/Lesson values lost to master-page name-collision on soffice merge
description:
Discovered while authoring the golden-reference integration test
(src/server/actions/assembleQuarter.integration.test.ts, task 6.2.14): the
real end-to-end soffice merge produces a book where EVERY lesson's footer
shows "Quarter 2 Lesson 99" (the TOC's own fallback value) instead of each
constituent's own lesson number. This is a genuine FR-004 defect distinct
from — and NOT caught by — the existing "footer not blank" unit tests
(flattenFooterFields.test.ts mocks the merge; assembleQuarter.test.ts mocks
sofficeAssemble). It is also distinct from the Pass 5/6 source-immutability
CRITICAL (that guard passes — sources are untouched).

Root cause (confirmed by inspecting the produced .odt's styles.xml): all 14
constituent .odt files (authored from a shared Word/LO template) use the
SAME style:master-page NAMES (Standard, Lesson_20_Content, Front_20_matter,
Body_20_Pages, etc.) — each with its OWN meta.xml Quarter/Lesson custom
properties. `flattenFooterFields` correctly bakes each constituent's OWN
literal Quarter/Lesson value into ITS OWN copy's styles.xml before merge.
But `sofficeAssemble`'s macro (Module1.xba, `insertDocumentFromURL`) imports
constituents into a blank base document one at a time; when an imported
document's page-style NAME already exists in the target (true for every
constituent after the first, since all 14 share the same names),
LibreOffice's import KEEPS the target's already-registered style and
discards the incoming one's definition — even though the incoming
document's body content still references that style BY NAME. Since TOC
(00.odt) is inserted first, ITS footer (Lesson fallback = 99, because TOC's
own meta.xml has no Lesson property) becomes the ONE master-page footer
definition used for the ENTIRE merged book; the visually-varying "title"
text people see per page is a separate LIVE `<text:chapter>` field
(resolves off the nearest body heading, unaffected by the style collision),
which is why the bug wasn't obvious from a glance.

Evidence: after a real assembleQuarter() run on the series-2 fixtures (test/docs/serverDocs/Luke-2-{14..26,99}v01.odt),
the produced .odt's styles.xml has exactly 4 <style:footer> blocks (not 14),
and every one of them (for the non-front-matter master-pages) contains the
literal text "Lesson 99" baked in — grep 'Lesson 99' matches on the footer
of every single lesson's pages in the rendered PDF, including e.g. Lesson
14, 15, 16, etc.

Fix direction (not prescriptive, but concrete): give each constituent's
page-styles (and every in-document reference to them, in both styles.xml
AND content.xml) a per-index-unique name (e.g. suffix each master-page /
page-layout name with `_00`, `_01`, ... matching the ASCII zero-padded
constituent filenames already used) before the merge, so the name collision
never occurs and each of the 14 footers survives independently. Alternative:
a post-merge rewrite pass that walks the merged document's page sequence
and re-applies literal Quarter/Lesson text per physical page based on lesson
boundaries — likely more complex given LO's page-style model.

Blocks 6.2.14's acceptance (yarn test:integration must pass); the test file
itself is written, typechecks, and its source-immutability guard already
passes — only the golden-reference parity assertion currently fails on this
defect. Do not weaken/remove that assertion to make the test pass; fix the
underlying merge instead.
close_reason: Fixed master-page name/display-name collision in soffice merge (FR-004); recreated assembleQuarter.integration.test.ts with all 5 parity axes + mandatory source-immutability guard, all passing against real soffice

2. Task graph was repaired: 6.2.14 (the golden-reference integration-test task) is now this task's PARENT grouping node and will AUTO-RETIRE when this task closes — it will NOT be worked separately. Therefore THIS task must deliver BOTH: (a) recreate + author assembleQuarter.integration.test.ts per 6.2.14's full spec (all 5 parity assertions: ordering, continuous page numbering, first-page suppression, footer Quarter/Lesson populated, page-offset parity; PLUS the MANDATORY Pass-6 SHA-256 source-immutability guard over all 14 constituents), AND (b) fix the underlying master-page name-collision merge bug so the golden-reference parity assertion passes. Do NOT weaken the parity or source-immutability assertions to go green — fix the merge. Opt-in via 'yarn test:integration' (real soffice, serialized).
