title: assembleQuarter: order resolution + copy-before-flatten (RED)
description:
**Spec**: specs/007-assembled-quarter-download/data-model.md §'Constituent provenance & copy-before-flatten (Pass 5 finding - CRITICAL)', specs/007-assembled-quarter-download/data-model.md §'Assembly order', specs/007-assembled-quarter-download/research.md §R2, plan.md 'Constituent Provenance — Source-Document Corruption (CRITICAL)'
**Skills**: test-driven-development
**Files**: src/server/actions/assembleQuarter.ts (NEW), src/server/actions/assembleQuarter.test.ts (NEW, soffice mocked)
**CRITICAL context a fresh implementer MUST read before writing any code**:
- `makeLessonFile` is reused UNCHANGED but has a load-bearing quirk (`src/server/actions/makeLessonFile.ts:15`): for the English-mother-tongue-bilingual case it returns `docStorage.docFilepath(lesson)` — the RAW ADMIN-UPLOADED SOURCE .odt under `docs/`, not a tmp copy. This is exactly the golden-reference case (English bilingual) the integration test exercises.
- For every other case (`makeLessonFile.ts:34`) it writes to `docStorage.tmpFilePath(...)` in the FLAT docStorage dir.
- **RULE: treat every `makeLessonFile`-returned path as STRICTLY READ-ONLY.** `assembleQuarter` MUST copy each of the 14 results into `<docStorage>/assembly-work/<jobId>/` BEFORE calling `flattenFooterFields` or the merge. NEVER run flatten or delete in place on a `makeLessonFile`-returned path — doing so for the English-bilingual case would corrupt or delete non-recoverable canonical source data (CRITICAL, Pass 5).
- **Naming**: name the copies ASCII, deterministic, insertion-order: `00.odt` = TOC, `01.odt`..`13.odt` = lessons ascending by absolute number. This is also what lets `sofficeAssemble`'s injected StarBasic macro reference only controlled ASCII `file://` URLs (a Unicode/DB-stored language name interpolated into a macro string/URL would break it).
- Generate each constituent exactly ONCE (do not regenerate for a separate completeness pass — Pass 1 finding on double-generation) — reuse the same generated path for both the completeness check and the merge input.
- Order: TOC first, then lessons ascending by absolute number (use the Quarter.ts helpers from the Foundational phase).
Write RED unit tests (mock `makeLessonFile`/`flattenFooterFields`/`sofficeAssemble`) asserting: correct ordering (TOC first), correct ASCII deterministic naming (00..13), each constituent generated exactly once, copy happens into the per-job dir before flatten is invoked (assert flatten/merge never receives the raw makeLessonFile path).
**Acceptance**: tests fail for the right reason; `npx jest src/server/actions/assembleQuarter.test.ts --runInBand`.

**Type**: RED
close_reason: RED: added 5 failing unit tests for assembleQuarter (TOC-first ordering, ASCII 00..13 naming, generate-once, copy-before-flatten into per-job dir, raw source never passed to flatten/merge); stub throws not-implemented, genuine assertion-level red confirmed via jest

title: US1: assemblyController — start/status/download endpoints (RED)
description:
**Spec**: specs/007-assembled-quarter-download/contracts/assembly-api.md §1,2,3,4
**Skills**: test-driven-development
**Files**: src/server/controllers/assemblyController.ts (NEW), src/server/controllers/assemblyController.test.ts (NEW)
**Context a fresh implementer needs**:
- Register in `serverApp.ts` beside `documentsController`, same access control as per-lesson download (no new authz).
- Path params: `:languageId` (number), `:book` (Luke|Acts), `:series` (number). Body/query `mode` ∈ bilingual|single-language.
- **POST .../assembly**: 202 jobId (queued|running) on start-or-attach; 409/422 status:'failed' on incomplete quarter; 429 {reason} (NO jobId, NO status:'failed' — client must branch on HTTP status, not body shape) when at the queue-depth cap, but attach-to-existing-job is NEVER capped (dedup-check runs before cap-check); 403 {error:'Forbidden'} when Origin/Referer fails `requireSameOrigin` (src/server/middle/requireSameOrigin.ts — mirror how invitationController applies it to its state-changing POSTs); 404 unknown language/book/series; 400 invalid mode, invalid :book (must validate against the Book union, don't trust the URL), or non-numeric :series/:languageId (must parse to finite integers, reject NaN rather than silently degrading to a 'missing everything' completeness failure).
- **GET .../assembly?mode=**: 200 {jobId,status,reason?,missing?}; 404 if no job for the key.
- **GET /api/assembly/:jobId/status**: same shape by id; 404 unknown/expired.
- **GET /api/assembly/:jobId/download**: 200 streams .odt with Content-Type application/vnd.oasis.opendocument.text and Content-Disposition carrying BOTH a sanitized ASCII filename= fallback AND an RFC 5987 filename*=UTF-8''<pct-encoded> parameter (language names are routinely non-Latin script — Arabic/Devanagari/CJK — a bare filename= cannot carry those bytes); strip CR/LF/quote chars before interpolation (header-splitting), percent-encode AFTER that strip; 409 if job exists but not ready; 404 if job unknown/expired OR the ready job's result file was already pruned by the 24h docStorage cleanup (handler MUST stat before streaming, never let a missing file surface as a 500).
- `reason` strings are ALWAYS drawn from a fixed vocabulary (e.g. 'missing constituent: Luke Q1 L6', 'a lesson failed to generate: Luke Q1 L3', 'assembly timed out', 'assembly failed (internal)', 'server busy, retry shortly') — NEVER raw soffice stderr / Node stack / absolute paths; log raw detail server-side only.
Write RED tests (Supertest-style controller tests, registry/action mocked) covering every status code above.
**Acceptance**: tests fail for the right reason; run existing controller test pattern (see documentsController.test.ts for conventions).

**Type**: RED
close_reason: Wrote RED supertest controller tests for assemblyController (22 cases covering 202/409/422/429/403/404/400 + download headers), stub controller returns 501 everywhere; tests fail for the right reason (status/body assertions, not compile errors).

- **POST .../assembly**: 202 status (queued|running) on start-or-attach; 409/422 status:'failed' on incomplete quarter; 429 {reason} (NO jobId, NO status:'failed' — client must branch on HTTP status, not body shape) when at the queue-depth cap, but attach-to-existing-job is NEVER capped (dedup-check runs before cap-check); 403 {error:'Forbidden'} when Origin/Referer fails `requireSameOrigin` (src/server/middle/requireSameOrigin.ts — mirror how invitationController applies it to its state-changing POSTs); 404 unknown language/book/series; 400 invalid mode, invalid :book (must validate against the Book union, don't trust the URL), or non-numeric :series/:languageId (must parse to finite integers, reject NaN rather than silently degrading to a 'missing everything' completeness failure).
- **GET .../assembly?mode=**: 200 {jobId,status,reason?,missing?}; 404 if no job for the key.
- **GET /api/assembly/:jobId/status**: same shape by id; 404 unknown/expired.
- **GET /api/assembly/:jobId/download**: 200 streams .odt with Content-Type application/vnd.oasis.opendocument.text and Content-Disposition carrying BOTH a sanitized ASCII filename= fallback AND an RFC 5987 filename*=UTF-8''<pct-encoded> parameter (language names are routinely non-Latin script — Arabic/Devanagari/CJK — a bare filename= cannot carry those bytes); strip CR/LF/quote chars before interpolation (header-splitting), percent-encode AFTER that strip; 409 if job exists but not ready; 404 if job unknown/expired OR the ready job's result file was already pruned by the 24h docStorage cleanup (handler MUST stat before streaming, never let a missing file surface as a 500).
- `reason` strings are ALWAYS drawn from a fixed vocabulary (e.g. 'missing constituent: Luke Q1 L6', 'a lesson failed to generate: Luke Q1 L3', 'assembly timed out', 'assembly failed (internal)', 'server busy, retry shortly') — NEVER raw soffice stderr / Node stack / absolute paths; log raw detail server-side only.
Write RED tests (Supertest-style controller tests, registry/action mocked) covering every status code above.
**Acceptance**: tests fail for the right reason; run existing controller test pattern (see documentsController.test.ts for conventions).

title: US4: completeness gate + curated failure-reason vocabulary (RED)
description:
**Type**: RED
**Spec**: specs/007-assembled-quarter-download/data-model.md §'Quarter completeness (FR-006 / US4)', specs/007-assembled-quarter-download/contracts/assembly-api.md 'reason hygiene', plan.md Red-Team Pass 1 'reason must be a curated message'
**Skills**: test-driven-development
**Files**: src/server/actions/assembleQuarter.test.ts (EDIT), src/server/controllers/assemblyController.test.ts (EDIT)
**Context a fresh implementer needs**:
- Series 1 is a REAL incomplete fixture in this codebase — missing lesson 6. Use it directly rather than constructing a synthetic incomplete quarter.
- Completeness = TOC present + all 13 expected numbers present (Quarter.ts helper from Foundational) AND each of the 14 generates via makeLessonFile without error. The cheap EXISTENCE check MAY run synchronously at POST accept (immediate 409/422 block); the GENERATION half always runs in the `running` phase (never synchronously in the accept handler — that would defeat FR-007's whole point of a background job).
- Failure `reason` is ALWAYS a fixed-vocabulary curated message (e.g. 'missing constituent: Luke Q1 L6', 'a lesson failed to generate: Luke Q1 L3') — NEVER raw soffice stderr, a Node error .stack, or an absolute server path. Log raw detail server-side only, never in the response.
Write RED tests: series-1 (missing lesson 6) → block naming 'Luke 1-6' specifically, not a generic message; a constituent that throws during generation → job ends 'failed' with a curated reason naming that lesson, no resultPath; reason never contains a stack trace or a filesystem path (assert via string content, e.g. no '/' path separators or 'Error:' prefixes leak through).
**Acceptance**: tests fail for the right reason.
close_reason: RED tests added: series-1 (missing lesson 6) block naming, generation-failure curated reason, and reason-hygiene (no stack/path leak) — assembleQuarter's generation-time gate now fails for the right reason, ready for GREEN (6.5.3)

title: Remediate: uncurated internal error messages leak into client-facing assembly failure reason
description:
**Review**: [sp:security-review], [sp:code-quality-review]
**Range**: master..HEAD
**Files**: src/server/actions/assembleQuarter.ts

**Finding**:
Information disclosure (server path / internal error-string leak) on the unauthenticated assembly failure path. In assembleQuarter.ts only makeLessonFile (lines 104-113) is wrapped in a curated, fixed-vocabulary catch. Three other throw sites are NOT curated:
- renameMasterPageStyles(...) call (line 117) — an execFileSync 'zip' failure or libxmljs2 parse error propagates its raw message (absolute paths, full zip command line).
- flattenFooterFields(...) call (line 118) — same.
- the empty-result guard (line 137) throws 'sofficeAssemble produced an empty result at ${result.outputPath}', embedding an absolute server filesystem path.

**[code-quality-review] Related correctness bug in the same block (lines 134-138)**:
`if (fs.existsSync(result.outputPath)) { ... check size ... }` only validates the output when the file EXISTS. If `sofficeAssemble` resolves (soffice exited 0) but never actually wrote `outputPath` (e.g. the macro silently no-ops), this guard is skipped entirely, no error is thrown, and `assembleQuarter` returns a path to a file that doesn't exist. The job is marked `ready` with a `resultPath` that isn't there — the download endpoint's own `fs.existsSync(resultPath)` check later reports "result has expired" (a misleading reason for a job that never actually produced a result). Fix this together with the info-disclosure fix below: both touch the same 4-line validation block, so do them in one pass, e.g.:
```
if (!fs.existsSync(result.outputPath) || fs.statSync(result.outputPath).size === 0) {
  throw new Error("assembly produced no result");
}
```

Propagation chain: AssemblyJobRegistry.promoteNext().catch uses error.message verbatim as the failed job's reason -> jobStatusBody() -> HTTP GET .../assembly and /api/assembly/:jobId/status responses -> useAssembleQuarter surfaces data.reason in the UI. The assembly endpoints are unauthenticated (same open posture as documentsController), so any network client can trigger a failure and read server paths / internal command lines. This defeats the module's own documented 'reason hygiene' control (comment at lines 107-111).

**Fix suggestion**:
Wrap the renameMasterPageStyles and flattenFooterFields calls (and any other unwrapped throw in the per-constituent loop) in a curated catch that re-throws a fixed-vocabulary Error, mirroring the makeLessonFile pattern. Replace the line 134-138 existence/size guard with the single missing-OR-empty check above (path-free message). Ensure no raw error/path/command-line string can reach the job reason.

**Acceptance**:
- No assembly failure reason returned by the status/download endpoints contains an absolute filesystem path or a shell command line.
- All throw sites reachable from the assembleQuarter runner emit only fixed-vocabulary reasons.
- A missing (never-written) `result.outputPath` is treated as a failure, not silently returned as a `ready` job.
- A test asserts that a forced renameMasterPageStyles/flattenFooterFields/missing-output/empty-result failure yields a curated (path-free) reason.
close_reason: Curated all uncurated throw sites in assembleQuarter (renameMasterPageStyles/flattenFooterFields catch, missing-OR-empty outputPath guard); added tests

title: US2: Template failure fails the job loudly
description:
**Spec**: specs/009-quarter-styles-template/spec.md §US-2
**Goal**: If the quarter styles template cannot be applied (asset missing/unreadable, or the style-load step errors), the assembly job fails loudly with a human-readable reason — exactly like other assembly failures — and no unstyled book is ever delivered as though print-ready.
**Acceptance**: 1) A missing/unreadable template asset ⇒ job ends `failed` with a curated reason, no document delivered. 2) A style-load error mid-assembly (corrupt template) ⇒ same fail-loud behavior, no document delivered.

title: US3: Swapping in the real template is a file replacement
description:
**Spec**: specs/009-quarter-styles-template/spec.md §US-3
**Goal**: When Chris's real quarter styles template file is received, a maintainer replaces the shipped asset file at `assets/quarter-styles-template.odt` and the next assembled book uses the new styles — no code change required.
